name: Add concepts + concept_mastery RLS USING policies before enabling CONCEPT_CAPTURE — RLS-enabled but no isolation policy (deferred via WI-688 EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED)
description: concepts + concept_mastery (packages/database/src/schema/concept-mastery.ts) are per-learner (profile_id UUID NOT NULL, FK profiles.id) and have RLS ENABLED (migrations 0107_gorgeous_cardiac.sql:50-51 + 0113_identity_stray_ddl.sql:114-115) but NO USING policy exists anywhere in apps/api/drizzle/ (grep CREATE POLICY.*concept = 0 results). WI-688 (PR #1554) added both to EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES in apps/api/src/services/database-rls-coverage.ts:155-156 — mechanically valid (cannot enter ALL_RLS_TABLES without a USING policy or the integration test breaks), but an untracked deferred isolation gap. A live ungated read route GET /notes/concept-mastery (apps/api/src/routes/notes.ts:167 -> getConceptMasterySignalsForTopics) already queries these tables; writes are gated by CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED=false (apps/api/src/services/concept-capture.ts:19) and tables are pre-production. RISK: when CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED flips true (post-identity-reset), read+write run against tables with RLS enabled but no isolation policy — recreating the exact blind spot WI-688 closes, shifted in time. Fix: add concepts_profile_isolation + concept_mastery_profile_isolation USING + WITH CHECK policies in a new migration, then move both tables from EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES to PROFILE_SCOPED_TABLES. This WI MUST be resolved before CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED is flipped true. Surfaced by WI-688 RLS-guard hardening verification (2026-06-28).
acceptanceCriteria:
Add concepts_profile_isolation + concept_mastery_profile_isolation RLS USING + WITH CHECK policies (predicate: profile_id = NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_profile_id', true), '')::uuid) in a new committed migration. Move concepts and concept_mastery from EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES to PROFILE_SCOPED_TABLES in apps/api/src/services/database-rls-coverage.ts.

ACCEPTANCE:
(1) Hardened RLS coverage guard (database-rls-coverage.test.ts) passes with both tables in PROFILE_SCOPED_TABLES.
(2) manifest->pg_policies integration test (tests/integration/profile-isolation.integration.test.ts) passes with concepts and concept_mastery registered.
(3) Negative isolation check: a row written under profile A's GUC is NOT readable under profile B's GUC; add this case to tests/integration/profile-isolation.integration.test.ts.

REGRESSION TEST (must fail without fix, pass after):
apps/api/src/services/database-rls-coverage.test.ts — 'EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES entries are not in ALL_RLS_TABLES' (fails once tables are moved to PROFILE_SCOPED_TABLES without a real policy migration).

HARD GATE: this WI MUST be resolved (policies live + tables reclassified) BEFORE CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED (apps/api/src/services/concept-capture.ts:19) is flipped true. Dependency is on CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED, not identity cutover. Deferred from WI-688 (PR #1554).
riskImpact: Per-learner concept-mastery data (profile_id-scoped) would lack RLS isolation if CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED is flipped before this policy migration lands; the live read route GET /notes/concept-mastery (notes.ts:167) already targets these tables ungated.
bodyText:
Completion Summary — 2026-06-29
What was done: Closed the row-level-security isolation gap on the `concepts` and `concept_mastery` tables — both had RLS enabled but no isolation policy (the policy was previously deferred under WI-688), leaving them open until a policy was attached. Added the missing isolation policies so cross-profile reads and writes are blocked at the database layer before `CONCEPT_CAPTURE` is enabled.
What changed: A new forward migration `apps/api/drizzle/0125_rls_concepts_concept_mastery_policy.sql` attaches the isolation policies (with its generated snapshot and journal entry under `apps/api/drizzle/meta/`); `apps/api/src/services/database-rls-coverage.ts` was updated to reflect the now-covered tables; and `tests/integration/profile-isolation.integration.test.ts` gained negative-path break tests asserting that cross-profile access is rejected (the WITH CHECK insert path asserts the database returns the insufficient-privilege error, and the USING read path asserts isolation inside a rolled-back transaction).
Verification: all required CI gates passed on the merged pull request — including the migration rollback-section gate, the ADR-provenance and decision-adr-link gates, the integration suite, and the merge-completeness invariant; the only red was the advisory non-gating Flag-ON identity-v2 job, which exercises an unrelated account-export test outside this change. The break tests fail without the policy and pass with it, confirming the policy is the enforcing mechanism.
Caveats / Follow-ups: the policy predicate was reviewed and approved before merge. A review CONSIDER note flagged that one `concept_mastery` WITH CHECK test seeds its parent concept row outside the rolled-back transaction and cleans it up with a bare delete, so an unexpected throw before cleanup could leak that row into later runs — this is the same shared-database test-isolation and row-accumulation pattern being addressed under WI-1153, and the try/finally hardening folds into that root-fix rather than this change.
